title: Team Attribution Values
description: Valid team_attribution values controlling which team a player-cell team RATE stat attaches to

_source_reference.note: The source file (param definition) and resolver (get_team_attribution) remain the authoritative implementation.

applies_to.note: Honored only for the two team RATE types. Counting columns and other rate types do not declare the param and are unchanged (see deferred extension below).

historical.label: Historical (team of record)
historical.description: The player's per-year team of record, resolved through the player_year -> team_year identity bridge (player_year_teams.team).
historical.mechanism: Routes through the player_year_teams CTE; primary team per (pid, year) by most regular-season games.
historical.use_case: Backward-looking analysis where a stat belongs to the team the player actually played for that season.

current.label: Current Team
current.description: The player's current NFL team (player.current_nfl_team), skipping the bridge entirely.
current.mechanism: Joins directly on player.current_nfl_team; the bridge is not materialized.
current.use_case: Forward-looking projection — a mover's NEW team's volume, e.g. a 2026 redraft view.

consistency_invariant.description: Numerator and denominator of a rate ALWAYS resolve to the same team; flipping team_attribution flips both halves together.

free_agent_current.behavior: Blanks both halves consistently. No last-team fallback (out of scope).

year_split_current.behavior: The single current-team snapshot repeats across year rows. Well-defined projection, not an error.

rookie_historical.behavior: Blank numerator (no bridge row); 'current' covers them.

caveats.cache_ttl_bug: A value change from flipping this param will not surface until the affected view's cache is manually invalidated/warmed (see task/league/data-views/fix-data-view-socket-cache-ttl-unit.md).

deferred_extension.description: team-stats-from-plays COUNTING columns and the select-string year-offset branch do not yet honor this param.
